assertions.title: Upload-time rebuild propagates the concrete single-auth runtime fields immediately

steps.expected: Test passes — priority, prefix, proxy_url, disabled, note, and custom headers are all propagated immediately
steps.observed: PASS (0.00s) — test completed successfully confirming all runtime fields propagated immediately

assertions.title: Uploaded priority affects auth selection without requiring a later reload

steps.expected: Test passes — selector picks the higher-priority auth immediately after upload without requiring reload
steps.observed: PASS (0.00s) — test completed successfully confirming immediate priority-based selection

assertions.title: Re-uploading an existing auth file updates propagated runtime fields without losing preserved timestamps

steps.expected: Test passes — ID, CreatedAt, LastRefreshedAt preserved while priority, note, prefix, proxy_url updated
steps.observed: PASS (0.01s) — test completed successfully confirming identity/timestamp preservation with runtime field updates

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. Upload-time runtime field propagation (VAL-PR2923-004), immediate priority-based auth selection (VAL-PR2923-005), and re-upload identity/timestamp preservation with runtime field updates (VAL-PR2923-006) all confirmed working correctly.
